evals.prompt: In my Expo (React Native) app I want a swipe-to-dismiss card: drag it horizontally, and if you throw it past a threshold it dismisses (calls onDismiss), otherwise it springs back. TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: A component using react-native-gesture-handler Gesture.Pan + GestureDetector driving a Reanimated shared value, springing back under threshold, calling onDismiss via scheduleOnRN from the worklet, animating transform (not layout), with a reduced-motion path and cleanup.
assertions: Uses react-native-reanimated shared values + useAnimatedStyle (not React state per frame) and animates transform/opacity, not layout props
Uses react-native-gesture-handler Gesture.Pan() + GestureDetector for the drag
Calls the JS onDismiss from the worklet via scheduleOnRN (or notes runOnJS is deprecated), not a bare JS call inside the worklet
Springs back with withSpring when under the dismiss threshold
Provides a prefers-reduced-motion path (useReducedMotion) that still dismisses but without the throw animation

evals.prompt: Add a collapsing header to a scroll screen in my Expo app — the header should shrink/fade as the user scrolls down. RN + TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: Uses Reanimated useScrollOffset (current) / useAnimatedScrollHandler with an Animated.ScrollView, interpolates header translateY/opacity from scroll offset on the UI thread with Extrapolation.CLAMP, and a reduced-motion fallback.
assertions: Drives the header from scroll offset on the UI thread (useScrollOffset or useAnimatedScrollHandler), not JS onScroll setState
Uses interpolate with Extrapolation.CLAMP on transform/opacity (not animating header height/layout)
Uses useScrollOffset (current) rather than the deprecated useScrollViewOffset, or useAnimatedScrollHandler
Mentions a reduced-motion fallback (static header) while content still scrolls

evals.prompt: I have a FlatList in my React Native app. I want items to animate in when added, out when removed, and smoothly reorder. TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: Uses Reanimated layout animations: entering/exiting presets on Animated.View rows + itemLayoutAnimation={LinearTransition} on Animated.FlatList, stable keyExtractor, and .reduceMotion(ReduceMotion.System).
assertions: Uses Reanimated layout animations (entering/exiting presets like FadeIn*/FadeOut and LinearTransition) rather than manual per-item animation
Applies itemLayoutAnimation (or layout) for reorder on the list
Notes a stable keyExtractor is required for correct enter/exit/reorder
Honors reduced motion via .reduceMotion(ReduceMotion.System) or equivalent
Does not animate layout-thrashing properties manually

evals.prompt: My Reanimated animations aren't running at all in my Expo project after install. How do I set it up correctly?
evals.expected_output: Guidance: Reanimated 4 requires the New Architecture (newArchEnabled); install react-native-reanimated + react-native-worklets; the react-native-worklets/plugin babel plugin must be last (auto via babel-preset-expo); use expo install --check; use a development build (not Expo Go).
assertions: States Reanimated 4 requires the New Architecture (Fabric) enabled
Says the react-native-worklets/plugin babel plugin must be listed last (and is auto-included by babel-preset-expo)
Mentions installing react-native-worklets alongside react-native-reanimated and using expo install / --check for compatible versions
Notes native modules need a development build, not Expo Go

evals.prompt: Build a draggable bottom sheet in Expo that snaps open or closed based on drag distance/velocity. TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: Gesture.Pan with a clamped translateY shared value, snapping to open/closed with withSpring using velocity, reduced-motion path, transform-based, cleanup.
assertions: Uses Gesture.Pan + GestureDetector and a clamped translateY shared value
Snaps to the nearest snap point with withSpring (optionally using gesture velocity)
Animates transform (translateY), not layout height
Includes a reduced-motion path (snap without the spring animation)

evals.prompt: Create an animated loading spinner using React Native Skia in my Expo app. TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: A Skia Canvas with a rotating Group driven by useClock/useDerivedValue (shared values passed directly to Skia props), a reduced-motion static fallback, and an accessibility label on the opaque canvas.
assertions: Uses @shopify/react-native-skia Canvas with shared/derived values passed directly into Skia props (useClock or useDerivedValue)
Drives the rotation on the UI thread without per-frame React state
Provides a reduced-motion fallback (static frame) since a perpetual spinner is exactly what reduced motion should suppress
Wraps the opaque canvas with an accessibility label/role

evals.prompt: I'm using Expo Router. How do I customize the transition animation between screens (e.g. a modal that slides up)?
evals.expected_output: Configure the transition on the route via Stack/native-stack screenOptions or per-screen options (animation, presentation: 'modal'), not by reimplementing with Reanimated; reduced-motion note (animation: 'fade'/'none').
assertions: Configures the transition via Expo Router Stack screenOptions / Stack.Screen options (animation, presentation: 'modal')
Explains navigation/react-native-screens owns the screen transition lifecycle (don't reimplement it with screen-level Reanimated)
Mentions cleaning up in-screen Reanimated animations on blur/unmount
Notes a reduced-motion option (e.g. animation: 'fade' or 'none')

evals.prompt: My React Native animation is janky and seems to block the JS thread. How do I make it smooth?
evals.expected_output: Keep transient motion in Reanimated shared values on the UI thread (no per-frame setState), animate transform/opacity not layout props, avoid runOnJS/scheduleOnRN in high-frequency gesture callbacks, mark callbacks worklet, profile.
assertions: Explains motion should run on the UI thread via shared values/worklets, not per-frame React setState on the JS thread
Recommends animating transform/opacity instead of layout properties (width/height/top/left)
Warns against calling back to JS (scheduleOnRN/runOnJS) inside high-frequency per-frame/gesture callbacks
Mentions the New Architecture / worklet execution or profiling (Perf monitor / Reanimated devtools)
